prompt:
Platform request. Our nightly Azure inventory job drives Resource Graph with a bash-wrapped az cli, and it's rotten in three ways: it silently caps at the first 1000 rows (nobody ever wired up continuation), it hammers the API in bursts until Azure throttles us, and last quarter a proxy bug replayed the same continuation token and the job looped for six hours. Rebuild it as a Go package, `rgexport`, stdlib only — module go-azure-resourcegraph-skiptoken is already set up and the acceptance tests (rgquery_test.go) define the API.

Research comes first, before changing code: use WebSearch and WebFetch to read the CURRENT official Microsoft docs — at minimum the Azure Resource Graph "Resources" REST operation reference (confirm the current GA api-version and the exact QueryRequest/QueryResponse schemas) and the official "work with large data sets" and "guidance for throttled requests" pages for Resource Graph. Only Microsoft first-party pages count as sources. Our pinned wire contract is docs/contract.json with provenance in docs/official_sources.json; both are protected fixtures. Compare the live docs against them and, if anything disagrees, say so explicitly in your summary without touching the fixtures or tests. Cite the exact official URLs you actually read in your final message.

Shape of the package (package rgexport at the module root; split it sensibly — request/response wire types, the paging engine, and throttling/error handling shouldn't be one file):

- NewClient(Config{BaseURL, Token, HTTPClient, Sleep func(time.Duration), MaxThrottleRetries}) *Client — every wait goes through the injected Sleep; nothing in the package may really sleep in tests.
- (*Client).QueryAll(ctx, Query{Query string, Subscriptions []string, PageSize int}) (*Report, error): POST the documented operation with the researched api-version, bearer auth, and JSON body — query, subscriptions, and options with the researched result format for object rows; $top only when PageSize > 0. Follow the continuation token exactly as documented: resend the SAME query and scopes with the token in options, keep $top, and stop when no token comes back. An empty data page with a token is not the end.
- Report{Rows []map[string]any, TotalRecords int64, Truncated bool, Pages int, QuotaWaits int}. Careful with the result-truncated flag — read the reference closely for its TYPE, it is not what you'd guess. Truncated should be true if any page reported truncation.
- Throttling, per the official guidance: every response carries the user-quota headers; when the remaining-quota header hits 0 and more pages remain, wait out the documented reset-duration header (hh:mm:ss) via Sleep and count it in QuotaWaits. A 429 (Resource Graph answers these with a RateLimiting error body) waits Retry-After seconds and retries the IDENTICAL request — same token, nothing dropped, no duplicated rows — at most MaxThrottleRetries times before giving up with the mapped error.
- Loop safety: if a response hands back the same $skipToken we just sent, stop immediately and return ErrRepeatedSkipToken (errors.Is-able) together with the partial Report of everything fetched so far.
- Errors: non-200s map to *GraphError{StatusCode, Code, Message, Details []GraphErrorDetail} parsed from the documented error body; errors.As must work; a 400 is terminal — no retry, no wait.

Acceptance: go test -race -timeout 30s ./... — the tests run a local httptest server, no Azure subscription involved. rgquery_test.go, go.mod, and everything under docs/ are protected; don't touch them.

research.interface: Resource Graph REST API, Resources operation, api-version 2024-04-01

official_sources.title: Resources - Resources - REST API (Azure Resource Graph)
official_sources.used_for: POST https://management.azure.com/providers/Microsoft.ResourceGraph/resources?api-version=2024-04-01 (current GA version), QueryRequest{query, subscriptions, options}, QueryRequestOptions{$top 1-1000, $skip, $skipToken overriding $skip, resultFormat table|objectArray}, QueryResponse{count, data, facets, resultTruncated, totalRecords, $skipToken}, the resultTruncated STRING enum, and the ErrorResponse{error{code, message, details[]}} shape

official_sources.title: Work with large Azure resource data sets - Azure Resource Graph
official_sources.used_for: the 1000-record default/max page size, paging by passing the returned $skipToken back with the same query and scopes, count < totalRecords as the more-data signal, and the conditions under which no $skipToken is emitted

official_sources.title: Guidance for throttled requests in Azure Resource Graph
official_sources.used_for: the x-ms-user-quota-remaining (int) and x-ms-user-quota-resets-after (hh:mm:ss) response headers, waiting out the reset window when remaining hits 0, the ARG 429 RateLimiting error body, and the stagger-not-burst guidance

research.language_rationale: Go is what our inventory tooling is written in, and the Resource Graph contract here is a single JSON POST whose hard parts -- exact QueryRequest shape, opaque $skipToken echo, string-typed resultTruncated, quota headers -- are wire-level semantics. net/http + encoding/json is the idiomatic zero-dependency surface; the official armresourcegraph SDK would only wrap this one call while hiding the throttling headers the task must honor.
